annotations.label: Homogeneous space (informal)

anchor.snippet: a homogeneous space is, very informally, a space that looks the same everywhere

annotations.note: Informal description; no dedicated Mathlib decl captures this prose-level intuition.

annotations.label: Homogeneous space for a group G

anchor.snippet: a homogeneous space for a group G is a non-empty manifold or topological space X on which G acts transitively

annotations.note: Mathlib defines pretransitive (= transitive on nonempty) actions abstractly but no bundled `HomogeneousSpace` structure combining nonemptiness with a topological/manifold X.

annotations.label: Symmetries of X

anchor.snippet: The elements of G are called the symmetries of X

annotations.note: Purely terminological; Mathlib has no dedicated 'symmetries' notion attached to a G-action.

annotations.label: G-space

anchor.snippet: X is called a G -space if it is equipped with an action of G on X

annotations.note: A G-space is precisely a type carrying a `MulAction G X` instance.

annotations.label: Homogeneous space (formal)

anchor.snippet: A homogeneous space is a G -space on which G acts transitively

annotations.note: `MulAction.IsPretransitive G X` encodes the transitivity condition for a G-space.

annotations.label: G-space structure as homomorphism

anchor.snippet: the structure of a G -space is a homomorphism

annotations.note: `MulAction.toPermHom` is the homomorphism G → Equiv.Perm X induced by a group action.

annotations.label: Topological G-space

anchor.snippet: if X is a topological space , then group elements are assumed to act as homeomorphisms

annotations.note: `ContinuousSMul G X` requires the action to be continuous, giving group elements as homeomorphisms when G is a group.

annotations.label: Differentiable G-space

anchor.snippet: if X is a differentiable manifold , then the group elements are diffeomorphisms

annotations.note: No bundled 'smooth/differentiable action of a Lie group on a manifold' class found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere as homogeneous space

anchor.snippet: Positive curvature: Sphere

annotations.note: `Metric.sphere` exists as a set but no homogeneous-space structure SO(n+1)/SO(n) is formalized.

annotations.label: Oriented sphere

anchor.snippet: Oriented sphere

annotations.note: No formalization of the oriented sphere as SO(n+1)/SO(n) found.

annotations.label: Projective space

anchor.snippet: Projective space

annotations.note: `Projectivization` defines projective space, but its description as PO(n+1)/PO(n) homogeneous space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: Flat (zero curvature): Euclidean space

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace` exists, but no formalization of E(n)/O(n) as a homogeneous space.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: Negative curvature: Hyperbolic space

annotations.note: Loogle finds only `Matrix.IsHyperbolic`; no hyperbolic space manifold or O(1,n)/O(n) presentation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oriented hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: Oriented hyperbolic space

annotations.note: No formalization of oriented hyperbolic space as a quotient of indefinite orthogonal groups.

annotations.label: Anti-de Sitter space

anchor.snippet: Anti-de Sitter space

annotations.note: Anti-de Sitter space is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine space over field

anchor.snippet: Affine space over field

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` formalizes affine spaces as torsors but the presentation as Aff(n)/GL(n) homogeneous space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Grassmannian

anchor.snippet: Grassmannian

annotations.note: `Module.Grassmannian` defines the Grassmannian functorially, but not as a GL/O-quotient homogeneous space.

annotations.label: Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: This includes Minkowski space

annotations.note: Loogle results for 'Minkowski' return only the Minkowski-bound number-theory lemmas; no Minkowski spacetime.

annotations.label: Line geometry of Plücker

anchor.snippet: the space of lines in projective space of three dimensions

annotations.note: Loogle finds no Plücker/Pluecker constants; the Klein quadric / line Grassmannian is not formalized.

annotations.label: Points correspond to left cosets

anchor.snippet: the points of X correspond to the left cosets

annotations.note: `MulAction.orbitEquivQuotientStabilizer` provides the canonical bijection between an orbit and G/Stab, the coset-space description.

annotations.label: Coset space is homogeneous

anchor.snippet: given a coset space G / H , it is a homogeneous space for G with a distinguished point

annotations.note: Mathlib equips `G ⧸ H` with a natural G-action via `MulAction.QuotientAction`, which is automatically transitive.

annotations.label: G-torsor

anchor.snippet: if H is the identity subgroup

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` formalizes principal homogeneous spaces (additive torsors); the multiplicative analogue is implicit via the same definition.

annotations.label: Stabilizers under change of origin

anchor.snippet: a different choice of origin o will lead to a quotient of G by a different subgroup

annotations.note: `MulAction.stabilizer` is defined, and conjugation lemmas exist, but the precise statement that changing the basepoint conjugates the stabilizer is a folklore consequence rather than a single named decl I located.

annotations.label: Stabilizer is closed; Cartan

anchor.snippet: If the action of G on X is continuous and X is Hausdorff , then H is a closed subgroup of G

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib lemma asserting the stabilizer of a point is a closed subgroup under continuous action on a Hausdorff space.

annotations.label: Clifford–Klein forms

anchor.snippet: double coset spaces, notably Clifford–Klein forms

annotations.note: No Clifford–Klein form construction is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stabilizer in line geometry

anchor.snippet: in the line geometry case, we can identify H as a 12-dimensional subgroup

annotations.note: Specific to the Klein-quadric example which is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic relation among minors

anchor.snippet: a single quadratic relation holds between the six minors

annotations.note: The Plücker quadratic relation is not formalized; no Plücker decls exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prehomogeneous vector space

anchor.snippet: It is a finite-dimensional vector space V with a group action of an algebraic group G , such that there is an orbit of G that is open for the Zariski topology

annotations.note: Loogle returns no 'prehomogeneous' decls in Mathlib.

annotations.label: GL(1) on one-dimensional space

anchor.snippet: An example is GL(1) acting on a one-dimensional space

annotations.note: No formalization of this specific prehomogeneous-vector-space example.

annotations.label: Minkowski space as coset

anchor.snippet: the space of cosets G / H is the Minkowski space

annotations.note: Minkowski space and the Poincaré/Lorentz quotient construction are not formalized.

annotations.label: Killing vectors bound

anchor.snippet: A homogeneous space of N dimensions admits a set of at least N and up to

annotations.note: Killing vector fields on Riemannian manifolds (in the geometric sense) are not formalized; loogle 'Killing' returns only Lie-algebra Killing-form decls.

annotations.label: Three non-vanishing Killing fields in 3D

anchor.snippet: homogeneous 3-spaces have the property that one may use linear combinations of these to find three everywhere non-vanishing Killing vector fields

annotations.note: Not formalized; depends on Killing-vector-field machinery absent from Mathlib.
